story_clusters.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and former US President Donald Trump met, with Trump jokingly stating "I am the boss" before the meeting. The two leaders shook hands, but there was no hug, marking the first in-person meeting between them in 16 months. The meeting occurred at the G7 summit.

articles.summary: The Jaipur ITAT held that reassessment proceedings were invalid because the Assessing Officer mechanically relied on Investigation Wing information without conducting independent verification. The Court observed that in the absence of any provision or guideline providing for promotion from a deemed date, no employee can claim retrospective promotion. articles.summary: The Supreme Court took 297 pages to say something Indian law has avoided for seventy years that a rescued woman is not the state's property. The judgment does not tinker at the edges of the ITPA. It rejects the statute's foundational assumption that rescue necessarily means confinement. articles.title: How commodity volatility is rewriting the rules of economic sovereignty
articles.summary: Commodity prices are becoming increasingly volatile, leading to a shift towards resource nationalism. This trend may have significant implications for India's economy and trade policies. The article highlights the need for strategic control over energy and other resources to maintain geopolitical power.
articles.raw_summary: For decades, the prevailing wisdom was that open global trade would moderate commodity prices and distribute prosperity. That consensus is fracturing. What is emerging in its place is something harder-edged: resource nationalism where strategic control over energy, rare earths, and agricultural inputs has become a new currency of geopolitical power, writes Dr. Arun Raste, MD & CEO at NCDEX.

articles.title: ₹10 overcharge on beer bottle costs KSBC ₹25,000:  DCDRC Holds That Charging Beyond Printed MRP Amounts to Unfair Trade Practice
articles.summary: A government-owned liquor retailer, KSBC, was fined ₹25,000 for charging ₹10 more than the printed MRP on a beer bottle. The court held that charging beyond the printed MRP is an unfair trade practice. This ruling sets a precedent for consumers to rely on the printed MRP as the statutory ceiling.
articles.raw_summary: A Government-owned liquor retailer cannot rely on internal pricing directions to charge above the MRP printed on a packaged commodity, the printed MRP is the statutory ceiling the consumer is entitled to rely on at the point of sale, and the burden to prove any R. 18(3) exception lies on the seller. articles.title: Conviction Cannot Be Denied Merely Because DNA Test Was Not Conducted: Srinagar Court Convicts Railway Staff In Minor's Rape After 14 Years
articles.summary: A Srinagar Court has convicted a railway employee for kidnapping and raping a 13-year-old girl in 2012. The court held that the lapse to carry out DNA profiling cannot decide the fate of the trial. This ruling has significant implications for sexual offence cases in India.
articles.raw_summary: A Srinagar Court Court held that the lapse or omission to carry out DNA profiling, by itself, cannot be permitted to decide the fate of trial for the offence of rape, and that the testimony of a prosecutrix in a sexual offence case stands on a higher footing and, if found trustworthy and reliable, does not require corroboration as a matter of rule, while convicting a railway employee for kidnapping and raping a 13-year-old girl in 2012. articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has ordered the premature release of Md. Rashid Khan, a life convict in the 1993 Bowbazar blast case, citing reformative approach to punishment. Justice Neena Bansal Krishna observed that Indian criminal jurisprudence has moved away from retributive theory of punishment and has embraced a reformative approach. articles.title: Health Ministry Ends Over-the-Counter Sale of Cough Syrups; Prescription Now Mandatory
articles.summary: The Health Ministry has made a doctor's prescription mandatory for cough syrups. This change aims to regulate the sale of cough syrups and ensure their safe use. The move is expected to improve public health and safety.
articles.raw_summary: With Drugs (Fifth Amendment) Rules, 2026, notified, the Health Ministry has withdrawn cough syrups from the Schedule K exemptions, making a doctor s prescription mandatory. articles.title: Fake GST Invoices Without Supply: When Section 74 Applies & When It Does Not
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has clarified that fake GST invoices without actual supply do not create a taxable supply. However, wrongful Input Tax Credit (ITC) utilization can trigger recovery under Section 74. This clarification is significant for businesses and taxpayers.
articles.raw_summary: CBIC clarifies that fake invoices without actual supply do not create a taxable supply, but wrongful ITC utilization can trigger recovery under Section 74. The distinction depends on how the ITC is used. The post Fake GST Invoices Without Supply: When Section 74 Applies   When It Does Not appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Quashes U/s 153C Assessments: AO Cannot Simply Copy Satisfaction Note
articles.summary: The ITAT ruled that the AO cannot simply copy a satisfaction note when assessing a person's income under Section 153C. This decision has implications for tax assessments and the independence of the AO's judgment.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that Section 153C requires the AO of the other person to independently assess whether seized documents have a bearing on that person's income. A mechanical satisfaction note based solely on another officer's communication was held invalid. The post ITAT Quashes U/s 153C Assessments: AO Cannot Simply Copy Satisfaction Note appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Madras HC Upholds Section 80IA Deduction as Audit Report Filed Before Assessment Completion
articles.summary: The Madras High Court upheld the deduction under Section 80IA for the assessment year 2009-10, ruling that filing the audit report along with the return was directory and not mandatory. This decision affects taxpayers who had filed their audit reports during the assessment proceedings. The court's ruling provides clarity on the tax deduction process.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that, for assessment year 2009-10, filing the audit report along with the return was directory and not mandatory. Deduction under Section 80IA could not be denied when the report was furnished during assessment proceedings. The post Madras HC Upholds Section 80IA Deduction as Audit Report Filed Before Assessment Completion appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Why Outstanding Bill Discounting Must Be Reported in Form DPT-3?
articles.summary: The Indian government clarified that only outstanding bank bill discounting amounts as of 31 March must be reported in Form DPT-3. This decision affects businesses and financial institutions in India. The clarification aims to provide clarity on the reporting requirements for exempted borrowings.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether bank bill discounting facilities require disclosure in Form DPT-3. It was clarified that only amounts outstanding as of 31 March must be reported as exempted borrowings, making the year-end balance sheet the determining factor. articles.title: Manamadurai Custodial Death: Madras High Court Asks State To Ensure Dignified Disposal Of Mortal Remains After Family Refuses To Receive Body
articles.summary: The Madras High Court has asked the District Collector of Madurai, the Commissioner of Madurai City Municipal Corporation, the Dean of Government Rajaji Hospital and other authorities to take immediate steps to ensure that the mortal remains of Akash, a 26-year-old who was allegedly killed in custodial violence, are disposed of in a dignified manner. Justice Victoria Gowri noted that the body had been remaining unclaimed for almost 90 days and the family had not been receiving the mortal remains

articles.title: Intel's new manufacturing tech enters initial production
articles.summary: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has changed his view on the 18A process, which could generate returns for external clients. This is a significant development in the technology sector. The change in view may have implications for the Indian government's policies on technology and manufacturing.
articles.raw_summary: Intel CEO ​Lip-Bu Tan has started recognizing 18A as a potential offering for external clients, reversing ​his earlier view that the ⁠process would ‌generate returns only through Intel's ​own products, ​finance chief David Zinsner said ⁠in March.

articles.title: Tata says India pollution board drops scrutiny of Apple iPhone parts plant
articles.summary: Tata Electronics, an Apple supplier, has cleared pollution concerns at its iPhone plant in Tamil Nadu. The state pollution control board dropped its investigation after Tata addressed issues regarding wastewater discharge. Farmers had complained about contamination of agricultural lands. Tata confirmed its facility's water samples showed no contamination. This development is significant for Apple's production diversification efforts in India.

Tata confirmed its facility's water samples showed no contamination. This development is significant for Apple's production diversification efforts in India.

articles.title: States' expenditure rises 131% in 10 yrs on welfare, development: CAG Report
articles.summary: States' spending surged 131 percent from 2015-16 to 2024-25. Welfare and development drove this growth. Revenue expenditure remained the largest budget component. Committed expenses and subsidies consumed over half of revenue spending. Total state expenditure reached Rs 51.20 lakh crore in 2024-25. Budgetary spending averaged between 15.78 and 17.49 percent of GSDP.
articles.raw_summary: States' spending surged 131 percent from 2015-16 to 2024-25. Welfare and development drove this growth. Revenue expenditure remained the largest budget component. Committed expenses and subsidies consumed over half of revenue spending. Total state expenditure reached Rs 51.20 lakh crore in 2024-25. Budgetary spending averaged between 15.78 and 17.49 percent of GSDP.

articles.title: PM Narendra Modi lines up middle-power talks ahead of Trump meeting at G7
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will attend the G7 summit in France. He will highlight India's expanding relationships with countries like Canada, the UK, and the UAE. These meetings precede his first in-person discussion with US President Donald Trump in over a year. The talks aim to strengthen India's global partnerships and economic standing.

articles.title: IHCL's domestic hotel demand strong even as Middle East crisis drags on Dubai business: Puneet Chhatwal
articles.summary: Indian Hotels Company Limited anticipates a year-long recovery for its Dubai hotels due to the Middle East crisis, though domestic business remains robust. Despite this, the company is on track for 12-14% topline growth, driven by its expanding capital-light management fee business and a strong pipeline of new hotel openings globally.

articles.title: 91% of Indian crypto investors avoid panic trading during market volatility: Mudrex
articles.summary: A Mudrex survey of over 6,000 active crypto traders found that 91% of Indian investors avoid panic trading during market volatility, opting instead for measured portfolio adjustments or patient observation. The study also highlighted rising long-term investing habits, conservative crypto allocations, and growing adoption of crypto SIPs across India.

The study also highlighted rising long-term investing habits, conservative crypto allocations, and growing adoption of crypto SIPs across India.

articles.title: Rupee touches six-week high on oil slump, RBI effect lingers
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened to a six-week high, buoyed by falling Brent crude prices below $80 a barrel and supportive central bank measures. Recent steps to attract dollar inflows, including tax removal on bond investments, have seen significant overseas investor participation. The rupee is expected to continue its upward trend amid optimism and anticipated inflows.

The rupee is expected to continue its upward trend amid optimism and anticipated inflows.

articles.title: $6 billion double dhamaka coming: Jio and NSE likely to file for India's biggest IPOs this week

articles.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit | Kotak's Nilesh Shah bets on India's electrification story over next decade; flags AI as the wildcard
articles.summary: India's energy self-sufficiency is a decade-long investment theme, driven by aggressive electrification to reduce import dependence. Experts highlight solar, storage, and nuclear power, alongside transmission infrastructure. The shift to EVs faces rare-earth bottlenecks, prompting domestic innovation. AI offers another significant, albeit different, growth avenue. Investors should manage thematic risks by scrutinizing receivables and diversifying across value chains.

articles.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit | Wealthy Indians are still way underinvested in global stocks, says PPFAS's Rajeev Thakkar
articles.summary: Indian wealthy investors must look beyond this year's market ups and downs for wealth protection. Experts advise a long-term strategy for lasting wealth. This involves reducing risks like inflation and taxes. Diversifying investments globally is crucial. Investors should increase their international equity exposure now. Focusing on business fundamentals, not just themes, builds enduring wealth across generations.

articles.title: Strong earnings, easing headwinds to boost market outlook: Devang Mehta
articles.summary: Indian stock markets are poised for growth. Strong company profits and stable economic factors are supporting this positive outlook. articles.title: Rupee continues to rebound: Currency jumps 31 paise to 94.29 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian Rupee strengthened significantly against the US dollar on Wednesday, driven by falling crude oil prices and a weaker dollar index. This recovery, fueled by expectations of a US-Iran framework agreement and potential capital inflows, has pushed the rupee towards the 94.00-93.80 zone. Traders are closely watching the upcoming signing of the US-Iran agreement in Geneva.

Traders are closely watching the upcoming signing of the US-Iran agreement in Geneva.

articles.title: From passengers to parcels: Noida International Airport to see first cargo flight today
articles.summary: Noida International Airport (NIA) is set to handle its first cargo flight, a domestic departure, just days after commencing passenger operations. articles.title: Ola Electric files application to settle Sebi probe over false information allegations
articles.summary: Ola Electric and founder Bhavish Aggarwal have approached Sebi to settle allegations of disseminating false information to inflate stock prices. articles.title: Oil prices near three-month low as Iran deal set to boost supply

Brent crude oil prices are below $80 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate is close to $77 a barrel. This development may have implications for India's oil imports and economy, but no specific policy actions are mentioned.
articles.raw_summary: Brent was below $80 a barrel after falling 15% over four days in its longest losing run this year. Meanwhile, West Texas Intermediate was close to $77 a barrel.

articles.title: India's rice stocks climb to record high; wheat inventories at five-year peak

India is well-positioned for both exports and domestic price stability.

articles.title: KIMS launches ₹1,500 crore QIP to repay debt and fund subsidiaries
articles.summary: KIMS has launched a ₹1,500 crore qualified institutions placement (QIP) through a 100% primary share issuance, with the indicative price set at ₹755 per share at up to a 3% discount to the last closing price. articles.title: World Bank, India discuss framework to assess land governance reforms across states

articles.title: Mumbai Mall to Save ₹217 Million Through Zero-Capex HVAC Retrofit
articles.summary: Commercial and industrial (C&I) consumers are increasingly looking beyond renewable energy procurement to reduce electricity costs, with energy efficiency upgrades emerging as another avenue to lower operating expenses.One such example is a commercial mall in Mumbai that is expected to save ₹217 million (~$2.29 million) over the life of its upgraded heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system through a retrofit completed without any upfront capital expenditure.
articles.raw_summary: Commercial and industrial (C I) consumers are increasingly looking beyond renewable energy procurement to reduce electricity costs, with energy efficiency upgrades emerging as another avenue to lower operating expenses.One such example is a commercial mall in Mumbai that is expected to save ₹217 million (~$2.29 million) over the life of its upgraded heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system through a retrofit completed without any upfront capital expenditure.

articles.title: Geon commissions 2.88 GWh battery storage project at Khavda, Gujarat
articles.summary: GEON, the future technologies division of Kabra Extrusiontechnik Ltd, has integrated and commissioned a 2.88 GWh battery energy storage system (BESS) at Khavda within five months. The project, among the largest utility-scale BESS deployments in India, is now fully operational.

articles.title: Govt to replace WPI with Producer Price Index, new series from June 15
articles.summary: The Indian government will replace the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with the Producer Price Index (PPI) starting June 15. This change aims to better measure price changes at the producer level. The WPI is currently used to track average price changes in a basket of commodities. The shift to PPI reflects an update in economic indicators used for policy and analysis.
articles.raw_summary: WPI is a key indicator for measuring the average change in the prices of a fixed basket of commodities at the initial stage of a commercial transaction over a given period, with reference to a base year.

articles.title: India Inc's sales growth gathers pace in March quarter, led by autos and metals: RBI data
articles.summary: Manufacturing companies saw sales jump 14.5 percent in the fourth quarter of 2025-26. Automobiles, electrical machinery, and non-ferrous metals led this growth. Overall, listed private non-financial companies achieved double-digit sales growth. Raw material expenses increased, indicating input cost pressure. Staff costs for manufacturing companies moderated. Services sector companies experienced a moderation in operating profit margins.

articles.title: India, Sri Lanka collaborate to strengthen trade interactions through local currency settlement initiatives
articles.summary: India and Sri Lanka are exploring ways to settle trade using their own currencies. This move aims to cut transaction costs and shield businesses from fluctuating US dollar values. The initiative will help Sri Lanka conserve its dollar reserves. Indian banks can now offer loans in rupees to Sri Lankan importers.

articles.title: Maharashtra advises farmers to delay sowing as monsoon rains fall short
articles.summary: Maharashtra is experiencing a significant rainfall deficit, with only 26% of its normal rainfall received so far. The government has advised farmers to delay sowing operations. Improved rainfall is anticipated from June 18 onwards in some regions.

Improved rainfall is anticipated from June 18 onwards in some regions. A heatwave is also expected in Vidarbha.

articles.title: India’s gold hunger stays unshaken as higher duties fail to close the import floodgates
articles.summary: India's insatiable thirst for gold remains resilient even in the face of increased import duties. Prior to the tax increase, there was a notable spike in demand, which has since sustained momentum. This pattern implies that heightened taxation is failing to temper the nation’s gold obsession.

articles.title: India's structural growth story remains strong despite global uncertainties: Ventura
articles.summary: India's long-term growth outlook remains robust despite global geopolitical uncertainties and intermittent market volatility, supported by resilient domestic demand, infrastructure investments and improving corporate fundamentals, according to financial services firm Ventura.
articles.raw_summary: India's long-term growth outlook remains robust despite global geopolitical uncertainties and intermittent market volatility, supported by resilient domestic demand, infrastructure investments and improving corporate fundamentals, according to financial services firm Ventura.

articles.title: El Nino to hit 9-10 states severely; Chouhan calls for district-level contingency plans for Kharif
articles.summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has directed district officials and agricultural agencies in 9-10 states to prepare contingency plans for the Kharif season due to expected El Nino impacts. The focus is on advance planning for rain-deficient districts, increasing cotton and pulse acreage, and providing scientific advice to farmers.
articles.raw_summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has directed coordinated meetings with district officials and agricultural agencies in 9-10 states likely to face severe El Nino impacts during the Kharif season. The focus is on advance contingency planning for rain-deficient districts, increasing cotton and pulse acreage, and ensuring farmers receive scientific, solution-oriented advice.

articles.title: Global commodity prices likely to remain elevated amid easing West Asia tensions; India's WPI seen at 7-8% in FY27: Report
articles.summary: Global commodity prices will stay high in coming months. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia and bad weather affecting crops are the main reasons. Supply chains will take time to recover even after conflicts end. This will impact energy, metals, fertilizers, and food prices. India's inflation is expected to average 7-8 percent in FY27.

articles.title: Cooling crude may ease inflation heat, give RBI room to stay on pause despite WPI surge: YES Bank
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation is high, driven by fuel and manufacturing costs. However, falling global crude oil prices and a stable rupee offer potential relief. This could allow the Reserve Bank of India to postpone interest rate increases. The central bank is monitoring weather-related food inflation risks. A new Producer Price Index framework is also being introduced.

articles.title: Indian soybean acreage expected to rise on higher prices, weak monsoon outlook
articles.summary: Indian farmers are planting more soybeans this year. Higher prices for soybeans are making them more profitable than corn. Forecasts of less rain also favor soybeans, which need less water than sugarcane and corn. This shift could boost India's soybean production and reduce the need for imported edible oils. Farmers are anticipating a good season for the oilseed crop.

articles.title: Fiscal stress deepens across states as all 28 post deficits in FY25: CAG report
articles.summary: India's states continued to face mounting fiscal pressures in 2024-25, with all 28 states reporting fiscal deficits and their combined liabilities rising to Rs 90.51 lakh crore as of March 31, 2025, according to the Comptroller and Auditor General's (CAG) report State Finances 2024-25 released on Monday.

articles.summary: India is adjusting its fiscal policy to boost domestic growth and economic strength. Global uncertainties are prompting a move towards long-term strategies. Measures are in place to manage rising energy and food costs. This shift aims to protect growth and reduce vulnerabilities in an uncertain world. India is balancing growth support with long-term stability.

articles.title: From Oman to Tanzania: How the Iran war is redrawing India’s trade map
articles.summary: India's trade is changing due to the West Asia conflict. Oman is now a major import partner, especially for energy. Russia and the US are also key import sources. Singapore has become India's third-largest export destination. Tanzania and South Africa are seeing increased Indian exports. These shifts highlight new trade routes and partnerships.

articles.title: India's export outlook is challenging for FY27, but worst is over: Elara Securities
articles.summary: India's export outlook remains challenging in FY27 amid moderating global demand and elevated electronics imports, but the worst phase appears to be behind as a weaker rupee, resilient services exports and strong remittance inflows are likely to cushion external sector pressures, according to a report by Elara Securities.

articles.title: India eyes biofertilisers after Mideast war stoked supply fears
articles.summary: Under a shed in north India, women scoop cow dung, lumps of unrefined sugar and flour to produce biofertiliser -- part of a growing effort to tackle anxieties over the supply of chemical fertilisers. India is among the world's largest consumers of chemical fertilisers, using roughly 63 million tonnes annually.

articles.summary: India plans further measures to attract foreign capital. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that additional steps will be taken if required to boost capital inflows. Receivables valuation will now consider credit risk, ageing, and economic factors.

articles.title: Hormuz may reopen, but fertiliser relief is still months away for India
articles.summary: Fertiliser supplies and prices will take months to return to normal. Natural gas and petroleum derivative supplies need to resume. Ammonia prices may stabilize soon. Sulphur prices could remain high longer. Disruptions to shipping lanes have impacted imports. Restarting plants and clearing stranded cargo will take time. India has enough urea for the kharif season but faces DAP pressure.

articles.title: India, UAE step up efforts to expand rupee-dirham trade
articles.summary: Over 15% of India-UAE trade has been invoiced in local currencies since the rupee-dirham mechanism launched nearly three years ago. Both nations are now intensifying efforts to boost adoption, aiming for faster trade and cost reduction, despite industry-cited structural bottlenecks. Authorities are focusing on onboarding more banks and simplifying procedures to enhance cross-border transaction efficiency.

articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation hit a record 9.7 percent in May. This surge was primarily fueled by rising energy costs. The new Wholesale Price Index series, with a 2022-23 base year, now includes more items. Experts anticipate a slight easing in June due to cooling global energy prices. Retail inflation also saw an increase.

Discussions will focus on finalising an interim trade agreement. The larger bilateral trade agreement will also be discussed. This meeting follows earlier talks between US and Indian officials.

Both nations agreed to strengthen defense cooperation. They also signed pacts for collaboration in digital technologies, labor migration, higher education, and audio-visual creation. Discussions explored joint ventures in AI and supercomputers.

articles.title: India, UK working to resolve issues for trade pact implementation
articles.summary: India and the UK are actively resolving issues impacting their free trade agreement. Discussions are underway in London to operationalize the deal. Key concerns include the UK's steel safeguard measure and carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism. These measures could affect Indian exports. India is also engaged with the EU regarding proposed sanctions on certain Indian entities.

articles.summary: India's trade with the US saw exports remain steady in May, but imports climbed significantly. Trade with China experienced a notable surge in both exports and imports. Several other countries also showed positive export growth. However, some nations registered a decline in exports. Imports from specific countries like Brazil and Peru saw substantial increases.

articles.summary: During May, India's gold imports skyrocketed by 34 percent, hitting USD 3.41 billion, as soaring gold prices fueled demand. In stark contrast, silver imports fell steeply by 86.65 percent, underscoring a significant shift in market trends.

articles.title: El Nino may hit India's monsoon, rice and maize output: FAO
articles.summary: India faces a potential threat as El Nino begins. The Food and Agriculture Organization warns of a weakened summer monsoon. This could stress vital crops like rice and maize during the kharif season. Farmers' livelihoods and food security are at risk. Early action is crucial to mitigate these challenges. The planet's warmer state amplifies these concerns.

articles.title: RBI releases final guidelines on marketing, sales incentives of financial products
articles.summary: RBI has prohibited third-party incentives to employees of regulated entities for selling financial products, while allowing banks and NBFCs to incentivize their own staff. New norms, effective January 1, 2027, aim to prevent aggressive sales and mis-selling, defining digital marketing intermediaries as DSAs/DMAs. Mis-selling will be assessed based on the customer's profile at the time of sale.

articles.title: Remittances, services surplus lift India to $4.7 billion current account surplus in April
articles.summary: India achieved a current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April. This positive shift was driven by increased inward remittances. The services sector also saw a surplus. However, the capital account turned deficit as foreign investors withdrew funds. Despite this, foreign direct investment saw a significant rise. Exporters have adapted to the global economic situation.
articles.raw_summary: India achieved a significant current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April. This positive shift was driven by robust inward remittance flows. The services sector also contributed to the surplus. However, higher imports, particularly crude oil, led to a merchandise deficit. Foreign portfolio investors withdrew funds amid global conflicts, impacting the capital account.

articles.title: India's trade with West Asia gradually improving: Commerce Secy
articles.summary: India's trade with West Asia faced disruptions in May due to the US-Iran conflict. Exports saw a slight dip but are recovering. Imports also declined significantly. The conflict impacted shipping routes like the Strait of Hormuz. Despite challenges, Indian exporters are utilizing Omani ports. A peace deal between the US and Iran is anticipated to ease trade further.

A peace deal between the US and Iran is anticipated to ease trade further.

articles.title: Unemployment rate hits 11-month high of 5.5% in May
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate climbed to a high in May. Rural joblessness saw a significant rise, reaching a 12-month peak. Urban unemployment eased slightly, particularly for women. Labour force participation and employment rates also saw a moderation. Youth unemployment edged up. These shifts suggest a softening in labour market conditions.

These shifts suggest a softening in labour market conditions.

articles.title: India introduces PPI for goods and services, begins shift from WPI
articles.summary: India has launched Producer Price Index data for goods and services. This move aims to provide a clearer picture of price changes from a producer's viewpoint. The government plans to phase out Wholesale Price Index numbers within five years. This aligns with international practices and IMF recommendations. The new PPI will offer better insights into inflation experienced by producers.

articles.title: Ladakh LG approves constitution of Snow Leopard and High-Altitude Nature Conservation Society
articles.summary: The LG of Ladakh has approved the constitution of the Snow Leopard and High-Altitude Nature Conservation Society. The society will be chaired by the LG, with several officials as ex-officio members. This move aims to conserve the snow leopard and its habitat in the region.
articles.raw_summary: The LG of Ladakh will be the chairman of the society, while the chief secretary, Ladakh MP, chief executive councillor, LAHDC, Leh, the chief executive councillor, LAHDC, Kargil, and the principal secretary, Department of Forest, Ecology and Environment, will be the ex-officio members, an official spokesperson said.

articles.title: SEBI allows AIFs to retain liquidation proceeds beyond fund life

articles.title: A single 3.3 kg mango from Madhya Pradesh sold for ₹3,800 — why is it so expensive?
articles.summary: A rare and expensive mango variety from Madhya Pradesh has been sold for ₹3,800. The Noorjahan mango is known for its unusual size and premium demand. Its limited availability and short harvest window contribute to its high price.
articles.raw_summary: Grown in limited quantities in Alirajpur’s Katthiwada region, the Noorjahan mango is known for its unusual size and premium demand. The variety attracts buyers every season due to its jumbo weight, short harvest window and limited availability.

articles.title: Delhi govt to introduce EMI model for ₹400-crore smart streetlight project: PWD minister
articles.summary: Delhi government will introduce an EMI-based payment model for its ₹473 crore streetlight modernisation project, aimed at replacing around 93,000 streetlights with smart LEDs to cut power costs and improve real-time monitoring, while expected savings of about ₹300 crore over five years will be linked to contractor performance.

articles.title: Brent crude oil falls below $81/barrel for first time since March: What's behind the move?
articles.summary: Brent crude oil prices have fallen below $81 per barrel for the first time since March. This drop is attributed to traders unwinding the risk premium built up during the U.S.-Iran conflict. The impact of this price drop on India's energy market is yet to be seen.
articles.raw_summary: The global benchmark has now fallen for a fourth straight session, after dropping nearly 5% on Monday and around 6% over the past week, as traders continued to unwind the risk premium that had built up during the recent U.S.-Iran conflict.

The sale is expected to raise funds for the government, which can be used for various purposes.

The rise in green deposits is expected to be deployed for clean transport and renewable energy.

articles.summary: A truce in West Asia may lead to increased oil supplies, but it could take time for consumers to see lower fuel prices in India. This could have implications for India's energy policy and consumer prices.

articles.title: India to remain fastest-growing major economy at 6.6% in FY27, down from 7.2% last year: World Bank

The report said domestic consumption remains resilient, particularly in rural areas, while GST rate cuts could support spending. Growth is forecast to recover to 7.2% in FY28, aided by stronger domestic demand, export growth, lower US tariffs and free trade agreements.

articles.title: The secret to keeping a family business alive across generations
articles.summary: Finding a successor is only part of the challenge for family-run businesses. The bigger task is creating systems, governance structures and professional management that allow the company to thrive beyond its founder. As more Indian family businesses enter their second, third and fourth generations, succession is increasingly becoming a test of institution-building rather than inheritance.

This move aims to clarify the legal provisions governing Aadhaar's use.

Despite these headwinds, P G remains optimistic about India's medium-term economic outlook.

articles.title: Yum Brands sells Pizza Hut in $2.7bn deal
articles.summary: Yum Brands is divesting its struggling Pizza Hut chain in a $2.7 billion deal. Private equity firm LongRange Capital will acquire Pizza Hut's global operations, excluding China, for approximately $1.5 billion. India's Pizza Hut business will also fall under LongRange Capital's management. The China operations are being sold to Yum China Holdings for about $1.2 billion.

articles.title: India in talks with Russia-linked Siberian deposit to source rare earth Samples: Report
articles.summary: India is seeking rare earth samples from Russia. This move aims to diversify critical mineral supplies away from China. State miner IREL is in talks with Rosneft for samples from Siberia's Tomtor deposit. India will study the mineral composition before further engagement. This effort supports India's growing demand for rare earths in electric vehicles and clean energy.

articles.title: The Rs 30,000 question: What is a homemaker’s work worth?
articles.summary: India's Supreme Court has recognized "loss of domestic care" as compensable, valuing homemakers' unpaid labor at Rs 30,000 monthly. This acknowledges the significant economic contribution of women's household work, estimated to be a substantial portion of GDP. Time-use surveys reveal a stark gender gap, with women dedicating far more hours to domestic duties, a disparity rooted in societal norms.
